name: Dancin' On Air
overview: Dancin' On Air was a 1980s television dance music reality show, forerunner of the TV show Dance Party USA. Both shows were produced and created by Michael Nise and his father Frank. The show started with US$100,000 from a small group of investors that included The Tonight Show Band leader Doc Severinsen. The program earned a 128% return on investment within the first six months. Dancin' On Air was produced from Nise's studios and offices located in Camden, New Jersey.

trailers.title: Slow dance segment on Dancin On Air:   Run Away by 10cc w/ Andrew Gold backing up
